growth_records.medium: Sodium carbonate/bicarbonate-buffered soda medium (pH ~10), thiosulfate as energy source

up.method: derived from mu_max and growth yield

up.units: mmol/(g protein.h) (from 3.8 umol O2/mg protein/min, Tm. aerophilum, sulfide-driven)

sec.method: not quantified as rate in review

growth_records.cite: Dimitry Y. Sorokin et al. The Microbial Sulfur Cycle at Extremely Haloalkaline Conditions of Soda Lakes. Frontiers in Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2011.00044. [PMC3128939]
growth_records.snippet: Maximal specific growth rate 0.33 h-1 ... Maximal growth yield with S2O3(2-) 3.5 mg protein mmol-1 ... pH optimum 9.5-10.0. 100% rates were ... 3.8 umol O2 (mg protein min)-1 for Tm aerophilum.
growth_records.notes: Review Table 2 (genus-level summary of authors' own primary work). mu_max=0.33 h-1; doubling td=ln2/0.33=2.10 h. Growth yield on thiosulfate 3.5 mg protein/mmol. Derived thiosulfate uptake qS = mu/Y = 0.33 h-1 / 3.5 mg protein/mmol = 0.0943 mmol/(mg protein.h) = 94.3 mmol/(g protein.h) (per g protein, not gDW; no protein-to-DW conversion assumed). O2 respiration 3.8 umol O2/(mg protein.min) x 60 = 228 mmol/(g protein.h), specifically Tm. aerophilum (Fig 2, sulfide-oxidation-driven, 100% rate). pH midpoint 9.75 of stated optimum 9.5-10.0; temperature not stated. Values are review-aggregated, not newly measured here.
